decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.3°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective first step is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip, leveraging the primary recommended variable within safe bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity by 1.5x enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the local thermal gradient and peak temperature at the chip interface.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
High conductivity materials may be costlier or heavier in physical implementations, though this is a simulation study.
